why: the Game-of-Life library cannot express program!=architecture, feedback, or a held-out implementation (one preregistered development cycle, D-062). The mission forbids weakening the requirements to stay in Game of Life.

cells.reg: next = we ? data : self  -- a genuine directed CYCLE; the PROGRAM is its initial value

interventions.ABLATION: clamp to 0 for the WHOLE window (the analogue of destruction; a clamp is REVERSIBLE, so a brief clamp measures only a transient)
interventions.PULSE: clamp to 0 for ONE step (distinguishes memory from feed-forward)
interventions.CONTEXT: hold cells at 1 for the whole window, to open gates and expose structure behind them

demorgan.note: output-wire length compensates, so TOTAL latency is identical (26) and the measured causal graph is IDENTICAL (18 edges). Same A_TOPO, same A_TAU, same F, different M.

delay_edit: a DETOUR in the channel (jog out and back), adding 2*d cells while the gate row and the output wire length are held fixed. MEASURED clk->out0 = 26/27/29/31/35 for d=0/1/2/3/5. My first version pushed the gate DOWN and shortened the output wire, so the total latency was UNCHANGED -- a delay edit that edited nothing. The qualification bar caught it.
qualification_bar: two independent graph paths (declared wiring vs measured intervention) must agree
no hidden dependence on evaluator labels
